title: Understanding Bulk Modulus
text: The bulk modulus is a measure of a substance's resistance to uniform compression. It is defined as the ratio of the change in pressure to the fractional change in volume.

title: Volume Change and Pressure
text: The change in pressure can be calculated using the formula: \( \Delta P $$=$$ -B \frac{\Delta V}{V} \), where \( B \) is the bulk modulus, \( \Delta V \) is the change in volume, and \( V \) is the original volume.

title: Calculating Change in Pressure
text: Given that the volume increases by $$0.20\%$$, \( \frac{\Delta V}{V} $$=$$ $$0.002$$ \). Use this to find \( \Delta P \).

title: Calculate \( \Delta P \)
text: Use the formula \( \Delta P $$=$$ -B \frac{\Delta V}{V} \) with \( B $$=$$ $$1.8\times{10}^9$$ \) N/m² and \( \frac{\Delta V}{V} $$=$$ $$0.002$$ \).

title: Force and Pressure Relationship
text: The force exerted by the juice can be calculated using the formula: \( F $$=$$ P \times A \), where \( P \) is the pressure and \( A \) is the area.

title: Calculate Force per Square Centimeter
text: Convert the pressure \( \Delta P \) from N/m² to N/cm² and calculate the force per square centimeter.
